description: Wrapping async UI in Suspense but no error boundary, so a rejected resource throws and unmounts a large part of the tree.
body: Failure mode: a lazy or data-fetching subtree has a Suspense fallback but no error boundary, so when the resource rejects the error propagates upward and blanks the page instead of showing recovery UI. Detection signals: a single failed fetch white-screens a route; uncaught errors in the console; no error boundary in the component tree above the async leaf. Do not rely on Suspense alone to handle failures; it handles pending, not errors. Safer fallback: add an error boundary at the same or finer granularity as the Suspense boundary, render a retry/fallback UI, and verify with a forced rejection.
